data.title: Rumble announces cloud agreement with government of El Salvador
data.description:
• Rumble (RUM) said on Friday that it has entered a cloud services agreement with the Government of El Salvador. 
  • Rumble’s services to El Salvador will include cloud storage, cloud computing, databases, load balancers, and Kubernetes integration.
  • Rumble (RUM) shares were up more than 11% in postmarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Aspargo Labs files to go public via direct offering
data.description:
Aspargo Labs (AAGO), which develops liquid formulations of solid dose drugs and drug delivery devices, has filed to go public via a direct offering.

Shareholders plan to offer up to 177M shares for the offering. The company will not receive any proceeds from the deal. In a private transaction in Q3 2024, 21M common shares were sold for $0.87 per share.

Aspargo (AAGO) hopes to list its shares on NYSE under the symbol AAGO.

Based in New York, Aspargo's (AAGO) lead product is a liquid formulation of sildenafil citrate, the active ingredient in Viagra, that's administered via a device that allows for customized dosing. The product is approved in Spain, the United Kingdom, Germany, the Netherlands and Ireland.

data.title: Brewers turn defensive as Constellation sounds sobering alarm on demand
data.description:
Brewer and spirit stocks suffered steep losses on Friday after Constellation Brands (STZ) warned of “subdued” spending and value seeking behavior among legal-age consumers, sending shares of Modelo’s parent company down by double-digits, and dragging down the sector in sympathy.

Although Constellation (STZ) said its beer business continued to outperform all categories, CEO Bill Newlands reiterated on the company’s Q3 earnings call that “subdued overall spending behaviors from consumers have been key near-term limiting factors on demand growth along with value seeking behavior,” a sentiment that spooked investors and weighed on shares of The Boston Beer Company (SAM), Anheuser-Busch (BUD), Molson Coors Beverage (TAP), Ambev (ABEV), Brown-Forman (BF-A, BF-B), Diageo (DEO), and MGP Ingredients (MGPI), all of which closed on Friday with losses ranging from 2% to 7%.

The loss was particularly pronounced for shares of The Boston Beer Company (SAM) which shed 7% on Friday, making it the fifth day in a row the stock has closed in the red and its fourth consecutive week lower. The company has struggled with lower shipping volumes and sluggish demand for its Truly Hard Seltzer, and is expected to post a loss of $1.27 per share when it reports Q4 results on February 11. Shares for the parent company of Sam Adams are down 22% year-over-year.

And it's not just Boston Beer (SAM) facing challenges. According to the Brewers’ Association, beer consumption per capita was down in 2024, especially for craft brewers as customers shifted to less expensive and more consistent brands, making last year the eighth consecutive year of declining consumption. Demand for non-alcoholic brands is also stealing market share as its popularity gains traction among younger, health-conscious consumers. Industry data estimates demand for non-alcoholic, or reduced alcohol beer to increase at a CAGR of 7% over the next 10 years and likely grow further on the heels of a Surgeon General advisory on the link between alcohol and cancer.

The warning spooked investors and sent beer, wine, and spirit shares lower in its wake.

In the aftermath of Q3 results, shares of Constellation Brands (STZ) fell 17%, making Friday its worst day in a decade.

data.title: NioCorp Developments reports preliminary net loss of $0.5 million
data.description:
• NioCorp Developments (NASDAQ:NB) on Friday reported a preliminary loss of $0.5 million, or $0.01 per share, for the three-month period ended December 31, 2024, compared to a loss of $3.3 million, or $0.09 per share, in the same period last year.
  • Preliminary net loss for the six-month period ended December 31, 2024, of $2.6 million, or $0.06 per share, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Matinas BioPharma receives NYSE noncompliance notice
data.description:
• Matinas BioPharma (NYSE:MTNB) said on Friday that it has received an NYSE American notice indicating that the company has fallen below certain NYSE American continued listing standards because it failed to hold its annual meeting of stockholders for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024.
  • The company intends to hold its annual meeting no later than December 31, 2025 to regain compliance under the NYSE American Company Guide.
  • Matinas BioPharma (NYSE:MTNB) shares were down 3.21% in post market trading on Friday.

data.title: General European Strategic Investments announces passing of CEO Wolfgang Rauball
data.description:
• General European Strategic Investments (OTCPK:GESI) on Friday announced the passing of CEO Wolfgang Rauball.
  • The company said its board of directors has begun the process of identifying a suitable successor to lead General European Strategic Investments.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boot Barn reports prelim third quarter net sales of $608.2M
data.description:
• Boot Barn Holdings (NYSE:BOOT) announced on Friday preliminary results for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025.
  • For the third quarter, the company expects to report net sales of approximately $608.2 million, representing growth of 16.9% over the prior year.
  • It expects to report same store sales growth of approximately 8.6%, with retail store same store sales growth of approximately 8.2% and e-commerce same store sales growth of approximately 11.1%.
  • Q3 prelim net income per diluted share of approximately $2.43 compared to net income per diluted share of $1.81 in the prior-year period. Included in net income per diluted share is an estimated $0.22 benefit related to the CEO transition.
  • The company opened 13 new stores in the third quarter, or 39 stores year-to-date, bringing its total store count to 438.
  • The company also said that it has appointed Jonathon D. Kosoff to serve as its Chief Digital Officer, effective January 27, 2025.

data.title: CMS proposes 4.33% increase for Medicare Advantage plans
data.description:
Shares of health insurers advanced Friday after the U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services announced it has proposed raising government payments to Medicare Advantage plans by an average of $4.33%.

Humana (HUM) shares jumped 6% in post-market trading, with shares of UnitedHealth Group (UNH) gaining 4% and CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) advancing 3%. Shares of Cigna (CI) were up 1% while Centene (CNC) shares were trading largely flat.

The proposal would increase payments to plan providers by around $21B in 2026 over 2025, according to a CMS statement.

CMS will accept public comments on the proposal through Feb. 10.

UnitedHealth (UNH) accounted for 29% of MA plan enrollment in 2024, followed by Humana (HUM) with 18%. In approximately 29% of U.S. counties, the two companies accounted for at least 75% of MA enrollment, according to the Kaiser Family Foundation.

data.title: Cabot declares $0.43 dividend
data.description:
• Cabot (NYSE:CBT) declares $0.43/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.98%
  • Payable March 14; for shareholders of record Feb. 28; ex-div Feb. 28.
  • See CBT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Baymount announces termination of private placement
data.description:
• Baymount (BYM.H:CA) on Friday said the company has terminated its previously announced private placement of units for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $100,000 through the issuance of units at $0.01 per unit.
  • BYM.H:CA closed -50.0% at $0.005.
  • Source: Press Release
